content.full_text:
Law Offices of

OLSON
CONNELLY
HAGEL
FONG &
LEIDIGH

FPPC

Oct 29 4:19 PM '92

October 26, 1992


Ms. Alice Hughes
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Alice:

    This letter is sent to confirm telephone advice
you provided to me. My client is the sole sponsor of a
statewide ballot measure committee. During the late
contribution reporting period, my client provided the
use of its nonprofit mail permit to the committee for a
large campaign mailing. The committee filed a late
contribution report reporting the receipt of the use of
the permit valued at the difference between the
nonprofit mail postage and regular bulk rate postage.
I asked whether my client was required to file an
additional report reporting the making of the
contribution as well.

    I was advised that, so long as my client did not
otherwise qualify as a committee, it was not required
to file a late contribution report. In particular, I
was directed to FPPC Regulation 18419 which spells out
when the sponsor of a committee does not incur filing
obligations as a committee.

    Thank you for your time and attention to this
matter.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH


DIANE M. FISHBURN

DMF:jlh

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH
GEORGE W. WATERS
DIANE M. FISHBURN


Of Counsel
LLOYD G. CONNELLY


CapitolBank Center
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Telephone [916] 442-2952
Facsimile [916] 442-1280

The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with very faint markings or lines. There is no clearly legible text content visible to transcribe.
